channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dead fox near Ha-Ha Road (crossing into Circular Way) SE18, 29th March
item.description:
There is a dead fox in Woolwich near Ha-Ha Road (crossing into Circular Way). What3Words: ///lake.hangs.dwell

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ha Ha Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped food containers, 29th March
item.description:
Dumped food containers

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rattray Road, Brixton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Accumulated waste – public health risk (pest activity), 29th March
item.description:
I am reporting a persistent and worsening waste accumulation issue at 9 Bell Yard Mews, SE1. For over two weeks, waste has not been collected despite the property being on a contracted waste service (First Mile) and despite repeated reports to the contractor. As a result, bins are now overflowing and waste is accumulating externally around the bin area. This ongoing failure is creating unsanitary conditions and has led to visible pest activity (mice) in and around the property, representing a clear public health concern. We have made repeated attempts to resolve this directly with the waste contractor over an extended period, however no effective action has been taken and the issue remains unresolved. This is now a persistent environmental and public health issue rather than a missed collection, and we request an Environmental Health inspection and appropriate intervention. We are attaching evidence including photographs and correspondence with the waste contractor demonstrating repeated failed attempts to resolve the issue.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 151,153, Bermondsey Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: E-bike on footpath, 29th March
item.description:
Hazard to blind, partially-sighted, disabled and vulnerable pedestrians

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ardgowan Road, Catford South, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Poor street cleaning, 29th March
item.description:
Rubbish, bottles, cans and takeaway containers continue to litter the street, for weeks, with street cleaners failing to pick up - cleaning is very superficial and highly ineffective. We deserve far better considering the council tax we pay. Enfield must do better.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Park Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress and other rubbish, 29th March
item.description:
There is an abandoned mattress and other rubbish against a house fence
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Slip, Plaistow, Greater London

item.title: Household items dumped in bushes, 29th March

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Edison Grove, Plumstead

item.title: Pothole, Crooms Hill, 29th March
item.description:
This has been reported to the council. Large pothole in the centre of road at lower part of Crooms Hill. Due to parked vehicles on one side, traffic has to pass over. Difficult to avoid and obstruction for all traffic (cars and cyclists). This could be dangerous for cyclists travelling down hill. Reported previously and also to Greenwich council but has not been repaired.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Crooms Hill, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road, 29th March
item.description:
Why on earth is this entire road getting retarmaced there’s no defects there’s plenty of other roads in local area that could do with resurfacing! Could someone get in touch regarding this!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 190, Chislehurst Road, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 💩 Poop 💩, 29th March
